task: Three boundary corrections from chairman — html-frontend is cross-platform fast-path (not a competing front-end), iching-oracle tech stack is the visual language for 'tell the Flwntier chief what to do', Android chief client runs the tarot E2E through Flwntier runtime (same UI, not a clone)
summary:
Chairman enumerated three boundary mistakes in a row. Recording them here so I don't re-commit the same misreadings, and patching the earlier NWTs where I wrote the wrong interpretation.

CORRECTION 1 — html-frontend vs desktop are NOT competing front-ends
* 'apps/html-frontend/' is the cross-platform fast-path for
  Linux/Mac. It's a vanilla HTML+JS client that talks to
  the same JSON-RPC on 8765. Chairman runs the desktop
  (Tauri NSIS, just works on Windows) — but a Mac/Linux
  user who only wants to use Flwntier without the Tauri
  packaging can run flowntier_runtime as a standalone
  binary and load the HTML frontend from a browser.
* Both are products. Both ship. The git tree has both
  (apps/desktop + apps/html-frontend) intentionally.
* My earlier framing — 'two equivalent variants' — was
  technically true but missed the point. The point is:
  desktop = Windows convenience, HTML = cross-platform
  fast-path. Same backend, different surface.

CORRECTION 2 — iching-oracle tech stack is a *visual language* for the Flwntier chief client
* The chairman's intent: '参考算卦的技术栈' (reference the
  tech stack) was never 'build a copy of iching-oracle for
  Flwntier'. It was: 'here is the visual language —
  Kotlin + Compose + Material3 + ink-and-paper teal — use
  it to issue a *spec* to the Flwntier chief'. Meaning:
  'I want the Android side of Flwntier to *look and feel*
  like iching-oracle because that's the language I've been
  using to talk to the chief.'
* The previous apps/ChiefApp/ was wrong in two ways:
    - Wrong function: I built a remote *controller* for the
      desktop runtime. Chairman wanted a *user-facing*
      Android app that runs Flwntier tasks (like the tarot
      draw) through the same visual language.
    - Wrong destination: I conflated the two. The Android
      app is NOT a Flwntier GitHub release (NWT 000071),
      but it IS a Flwntier code surface (events in the
      .nwt/ timeline, lives in the repo) — just not a
      published release product.
* So: chief-app code goes back, as an Android client for
  Flwntier, written in the iching-oracle style. Not a
  port of iching-oracle, not a controller, not a release.

CORRECTION 3 — Android chief client runs the tarot E2E
* Chairman: '现在我需要你让 Flwntier 跑一下安卓的塔罗牌,
  前端要完全一致,抽卡也要图片'.
* So the Android client:
    - Has iching-oracle's hexagram-draw visual idiom
      (centered card, animated draw, 3-card spread
      past/present/future, 3D flip, upright/reversed
      outcome, poetic interpretation text).
    - Calls Flwntier runtime (POST /api/run_workflow
      with user_request='draw a tarot card', or a new
      endpoint like POST /api/tarot/draw that wraps the
      orchestrator in single-draw mode) — i.e. the cards
      come from the *chief / orchestrator*, not from a
      local 64-gua JSON like iching-oracle does.
    - Front-end look identical to iching-oracle but the
      data path is through Flwntier runtime + 78-card
      dataset (which the chief just generated, or which
      can be a static asset shipped with the Android app
      for offline use — the chairman hasn't decided yet).
* This is the v0.2.0 (or v0.5.0) chief-app direction.
  The deleted apps/ChiefApp/ (event 000070) was the
  remote controller. We need a different apps/ChiefApp/:
  a Flwntier-running tarot drawer, iching-oracle-styled.

CORRECTION 4 — record + fix earlier NWTs
* NWT 000068 (orchestrator): the description framing was
  'build a tarot app'. Fix: 'validate that the chief /
  orchestrator runs the chairman's tarot end-to-end smoke
  test correctly'.
* NWT 000069 (PlanDoc segments + async): same framing
  fix.
* NWT 000070 (chief-app v0.1.0): the description still
  frames it as 'remote controller' which is now wrong.
  Fix: re-frame as 'a first attempt at a chief Android
  client; the function (controller) and destination
  (release) were both wrong; replaced by event 000073+'s
  tarot-drawer chief-app'.
* NWT 000071 (boundary corrections): the 'chief-app code
  stays in the repo as a tool' line is wrong. The chairman
  wants a real Android chief client (a Flwntier product
  surface, not a release), running the tarot. So chief-app
  comes back, but different.
* NWT 000072 (delete chief-app): this whole NWT is wrong
  because the deletion was based on the wrong reading.
  The fix is: re-add apps/ChiefApp/, with a different
  function (Flwntier-tarot-drawer, iching-oracle-styled)
  and a different release policy (NOT on the Flwntier
  GitHub releases page, since it's a tool — but it IS a
  Flwntier code surface that lives in the repo).
reason: Chairman correction — three boundary mistakes in one message. Recording each so future NWTs + future agent sessions don't repeat them. Patching the earlier NWTs so the historical record doesn't carry the wrong interpretation forward.
